steps.title: Spot the connection between alarm calls and danger
steps.content: The passage says that after hearing T. caesius alarm calls, C. cinerascens froze or scattered, leading researchers to conclude the birds link those calls to danger.

steps.title: Apply the link to the answer choices
steps.content:
B shows that only the alarm calls trigger the freezing/scattering response, while random noise produces no reaction, directly supporting the idea that C. cinerascens associates those calls with danger.
The other choices either do not test specificity, show the reaction occurring without the calls, or extend the effect to other species, which does not bolster the conclusion.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
